name: align_is_noop_for_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_is_noop_for_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: If alignment returns the original value even when the input is not aligned, the function fails its purpose.

name: align_changes_already_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_changes_already_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If aligning an already-aligned value changes it, the function incorrectly modifies valid inputs.

name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Downward alignment must never produce a value larger than the original input.

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of alignment to b should always be a multiple of b; violating this breaks all page-table address invariants.

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Alignment should be idempotent; applying it twice must yield the same result as once, otherwise downstream walk computations diverge.
